visualGoal: Teach a frustrated simmer to fix twitchy MSFS 2024 controls with a curve, and make the drift-vs-curve distinction unmissable. This is a low-product-density explainer: the real visual need is INFOGRAPHICS (settings screen, decision tree, curve shape), not product cards. Only genuinely relevant drift-free hardware appears, as proof for the hardware section.

infographicPrompts.title: The MSFS 2024 Sensitivity screen, explained
content: Sensitivity (response curve) — bows the center, keeps extremes
Neutral / dead zone — ignores input near center (drift only)
Extremity dead zone — trims the last 10-20% of travel
Saturation — how much virtual deflection full travel gives (~40% for 1:1)
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable labels, callout arrows to each slider on a clean recreated MSFS 2024 Controls > Sensitivity panel. IMPORTANT: recreate the ACTUAL current MSFS 2024 build screen — verify slider names against a live 2024 install before finalizing; do not invent a Reactivity slider if the build doesn't show one.
infographicPrompts.codexNote: Highest-value asset for this article. The body deliberately hedges slider names to 'tune what you see / check current' because primary sources disagree — a verified dated screenshot lets us tighten that language.

infographicPrompts.title: Twitchy or drifting? Two problems, two fixes
content: Does the aircraft nod on tiny inputs while your hands ARE on the controls? -> TWITCHY -> soften the Sensitivity curve (software fix)
Does it bank / trim creep with your hands OFF? -> DRIFTING -> hardware: worn potentiometers; small dead zone is a band-aid, contactless sensors are the cure
Both feel wrong? -> calibrate the physical range FIRST, then re-judge
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered/branching decision tree, IgnitionSim flight palette, big readable text. This is the article's core mental model.

infographicPrompts.title: Linear vs softened response curve
content: Linear: 10% stick = 10% surface, all the way (center as aggressive as extremes)
Softened (~-15% to -30%): gentle center, full authority at the edges
Why it matters: ~5 in of consumer travel mapped onto ~12 in of real column compresses every input
infographicPrompts.style: Side-by-side response-curve plot on navy drafting grid, cyan curve line, IgnitionSim aviation-blueprint aesthetic. No product photos needed.

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult flight-sim curator Mac Donovan at a tidy home flight-sim desk, one hand resting on a GA yoke, looking at a monitor showing a controls/sensitivity settings screen (no fake UI text), calm instructor energy, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait, space beside him for a settings-panel overlay.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: A real, dated screenshot of the MSFS 2024 Options > Controls > (device) > Sensitivity screen showing the actual current slider set.
screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.why: Resolves the article's central hedge (was Reactivity removed? is Saturation exposed?). Would let us replace 'check current' with a verified statement. Capture from a current MSFS 2024 build.

openQuestions: Does the current MSFS 2024 shipping build still label any axis slider 'Reactivity', or is tuning fully via Sensitivity + Neutral + Extremity dead zone? Primary sources disagree; the article hedges deliberately.
Confirm Fenix's ~-30% A320 sensitivity recommendation against a live read of support.fenixsim.com (page 403'd on automated fetch).
Is 'Saturation' exposed by that name in the 2024 Controls UI, or only via community tooling? The article mentions it as an option to verify.
Pre-existing routing gap (out of scope): this article does not emit an HTML page in dist/flight/, same as the certified reference best-flight-sim-hardware-2026. Flag to routing owner.
